meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build high-converting user onboarding and overcome 'no project ideas' syndrome. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcoming 'no project ideas' requires shifting from ideation to observation. By implementing friction-free user onboarding, you capture early-stage behavioral data. This data reveals user pain points, effectively turning your onboarding flow into a market research engine that generates your next high-margin SaaS feature or pivot point automatically.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. You stare at your IDE, waiting for a 'million-dollar idea' that never arrives, while your competitors iterate on the feedback they are actually gathering from users. Most founders fail because they build in a vacuum, treating products as static statues rather than living experiments.

The real secret isn't a better idea; it's a better feedback loop. By deploying a hyper-optimized onboarding flow, you stop guessing what people want and start observing what they actually struggle with. This isn't just about 'signing up users'—it's about building a diagnostic tool that validates demand before you write a single line of core product code.

steps.title: Define the Value-First Onboarding Hook
steps.description: Don't ask for email first. Ask for the user's biggest bottleneck related to your niche. This captures intent data immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for an onboarding wizard that asks three high-intent questions about user pain points. Ensure the state management is handled via Zustand and persists to local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx --prompt 'Implement the state machine logic for a 3-step wizard with form validation'

steps.title: Instrument Behavioral Event Tracking
steps.description: If you aren't tracking where users drop off, you are flying blind. We will implement middleware to capture every interaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom analytics hook that tracks user progress through the onboarding flow and sends event logs to a mock endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add a telemetry middleware to the onboarding flow that captures step completion time and drop-off points'

steps.title: Dynamic Content Personalization
steps.description: Tailor the dashboard based on the onboarding inputs to prove value instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a function that maps onboarding pain-point inputs to specific dashboard feature recommendations using a configuration object.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/utils/personalization.ts --prompt 'Refactor the dashboard loader to accept user profile data and return a personalized feature set'

steps.title: Frictionless Authentication Integration
steps.description: Use Magic Links or Social Auth to ensure the barrier to entry is near zero.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a Supabase Auth flow into the final step of the onboarding wizard, ensuring a seamless transition from guest to authenticated user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Connect the onboarding wizard submit button to the Supabase auth client with a redirect to the dashboard'

steps.title: Automated Feedback Synthesis
steps.description: Aggregate the onboarding data to highlight patterns in user pain points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js script that parses the collected onboarding data and outputs a summary of the most common user pain points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file scripts/analyze-onboarding.ts --prompt 'Create a script that aggregates JSON logs and prints a summary of user pain points'

pro_tips: Never ask for a credit card during onboarding; focus entirely on collecting high-intent qualitative data.
Use 'Progressive Disclosure': Show only the fields absolutely necessary for the current step to keep cognitive load low.
Implement an 'Exit Intent' modal that asks why they are leaving; this is the most honest feedback you will ever receive.
A/B test your onboarding headlines against the specific pain points you gathered in the first step.
Treat your onboarding flow as a product itself—it should be version-controlled and iterated upon just like your core backend.
content.next_steps: Analyze the drop-off data from your new onboarding flow for 72 hours. Use the 'Automated Feedback Synthesis' script to identify the top three pain points users reported, then build a micro-feature to solve the number one complaint.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn user friction into a data-driven product roadmap. Stop guessing and start building products that users are already begging for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
